title: Cinatra WP/Drupal assistant — bundle config block (v1)
description: Config object a v1 client (the `cinatra` WordPress plugin / Drupal module) injects for the bundle.js IIFE to read at boot. Per the v1 contract the WordPress plugin exposes it as the `CinatraConfig` JS global and the Drupal module as `drupalSettings.cinatra`. The bundle forwards `apiKey` (as a Bearer token) and `contractVersion` on every stream-init POST. `cinatraUrl` + `apiKey` + `contractVersion` are mandatory; the per-page node/post fields are populated only on the relevant CMS routes.

contractVersion.description: Pins the plugin↔core wire contract; forwarded verbatim on stream-init.

cinatraUrl.description: Base URL of the Cinatra instance, no trailing slash.

apiKey.description: Bearer token minted by Cinatra for this CMS connector.

instanceId.description: Connector instance ID resolving which CMS site the editor agent targets. May be empty until the operator configures it (Cinatra surfaces an admin notice).

drupalAdminUrl.description: Drupal admin base URL (Drupal only; may be empty).

nodeId.description: Current Drupal node id (Drupal node routes only).

nodeBundle.description: Current Drupal node bundle (Drupal node routes only).

nodeStatus.description: Current Drupal node status: published|draft (Drupal node routes only).
